sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To view the Organization Activity Feed, you must have [Organization Member](<Organization Member>)
access or higher to the organization.

To view the Project Activity Feed, you must have [Project Read Only](<Project Read Only>)
access or higher to the project.

sections.heading: View the Activity Feed
sections.body:
You can access events at the organization or project level.
You can use the Atlas UI, Atlas CLI, or the
Atlas Administration API. Select the following tabs based on how you would
like to access events.

Organization Activity Feed

Atlas CLI

To return all events for an organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas events organizations list [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas events organizations list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-events-organizations-list).

Atlas Administration API

You can retrieve events for an organization using the
[get all](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listorgevents) API (Application Programming Interface)
resource.

Atlas UI

To view and filter the **Organization Activity Feed** in the Atlas UI:

Review event times, severities, categories, and sources. Click an
event row for more information.

You can filter the organization activity feed by event type and time
range. You can combine filtering methods together for greater control
over the activity feed output.

Filter by event

Click Filter and select Event. To include
event categories or specific events, select is. To
exclude event categories or specific events, select
is not. From the Filter by event(s) drop-down
menu, select the event categories or specific events to include or
exclude in the activity feed.

Filter by event severity

Click Filter and select Event Severity. To
include specific event severities, select is. To exclude
specific event severities, select is not. From the Select event severity drop-down menu, select the
specific event severities to include or exclude in the activity feed.

Filter by event sources

Click Filter and select Initiated By. To
include specific event sources, select is. To exclude
specific event sources, select is not. From the
Select event sources drop-down menu, select the
specific event sources to include or exclude in the activity feed. To
filter by a specific user, select User and specify the
user email.

Filter by time range

Select a time range or configure a custom range to view events from
within the specified time range.

Select Copy URL from the Share drop-down
menu.

Scroll to the bottom of the feed. Select
Items per page\` from the drop-down menu. Click the
arrows to navigate between pages.

Project Activity Feed

Atlas CLI

To return all events for a project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas events projects list [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas events projects list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-events-projects-list).

Atlas Administration API

You can retrieve events for a project using the
[get all](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupevents) API (Application Programming Interface)
resource.

Atlas UI

To view and filter the **Project Activity Feed** in the Atlas UI:

Review event times, severities, categories, and sources. Click an
event row for more information.

You can filter the project activity feed by event type,
cluster, and time range. You can combine filtering
methods together for greater control over the activity feed output.

Filter by event

Select Event. To include event categories or specific
events, select is. To exclude event categories or
specific events, select is not. From the
Filter by event(s) drop-down menu, select the event
categories or specific events to include or exclude in the activity
feed.

Filter by event severity

Click Filter and select Event Severity. To
include specific event severities, select is. To exclude
specific event severities, select is not. From the Select event severity drop-down menu, select the
specific event severities to include or exclude in the activity feed.

Filter by event sources

Click Filter and select Initiated By. To
include specific event sources, select is. To exclude
specific event sources, select is not. From the
Select event sources drop-down menu, select the
specific event sources to include or exclude in the activity feed. To
filter by a specific user, select User and specify the
user email.

Filter by clusters

Click Filter and select Cluster. To include
specific clusters, select is. To exclude specific
clusters, select is not. From the
Select clusters drop-down menu, select the specific
active or deleted clusters to include or exclude in the activity feed.

Filter by time range

Select a time range or configure a custom range to view events from
within the specified time range.

Select Copy URL from the Share drop-down
menu.

Scroll to the bottom of the feed. Select
Items per page\` from the drop-down menu. Click the
arrows to navigate between pages.

sections.heading: View the Activity Feed
sections.body:
> **Note:** The Organization Activity Feed provides records of all activity at the organization level
> from the organization's creation to its deletion.

sections.heading: View the Activity Feed
sections.body: > **Note:** The Project Activity Feed provides project activity records.

sections.heading: Isolate User-Specific Activity with Custom Audit Filters
